claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have a child or children you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about?

Once I know how many children we're discussing, I'll need to gather information about each child and your relationship to them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I'm asking about one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'll help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for your son Alex.

Let me ask you some questions about Alex and your circumstances:

**First, how old is Alex?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Alex is 15, so he's under 16 which is good for Child Benefit purposes.

Now I need to ask you some questions about your relationship to Alex and your living arrangements:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship and responsibility for Alex:

**What is your relationship to Alex?** (For example, are you his parent, step-parent, or another type of guardian/carer?)

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Now I need to check a few more details:

**Do you have the main responsibility for Alex?** In other words, are you the person primarily responsible for his day-to-day care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do have the main responsibility for Alex. I take care of him day-to-day.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I just have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** (This will become relevant when he turns 16, but I want to understand his current situation.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment - he's just 15 and still at secondary school doing his normal schoolwork.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- You are Alex's parent
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You have main responsibility for his day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is attending secondary school

**Good news - you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!**

You meet all the key criteria:
- You're responsible for a child under 16
- The child lives with you in the UK
- You have main responsibility for him
- No one else is claiming for him

**Important note for the future:** Once Alex turns 16, he'll need to be in full-time non-advanced education or approved training for you to continue receiving Child Benefit (this can continue until he turns 20 if he remains in qualifying education/training).

Would you like any information about how to apply for Child Benefit, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for explaining that to me! I understand about needing to check when he turns 16. I think I have all the information I need for now. Thanks for your help!
